theme: tailor cutting one bolt of cloth for many patterns
title: Bolt and Braid: One Shop's Cutting Cards
seed_problem: vertical (column) partitioning of a record schema under a fixed query workload
mutation: composite: add a global field-replication budget on top of vertical-partition design, so a field may live on a second card; the objective's cover cost then depends on a per-query cheapest SET COVER over each field's (<=2) hosting cards (a flat per-card pull fee K plus a footprint-proportional charge), not on a hard partition, which changes which structure is worth building.
strategies: trivial: every component on one single card, no replication (reproduces the checker's do-nothing baseline)

naive greedy: mine RAW co-occurrence COUNT between component pairs and agglomeratively merge the strongest edges into cards under a fixed size cap, never spending the replication budget (mines affinity, but treats replication and traffic weight as irrelevant)

insight (strong): mine TRAFFIC-WEIGHTED affinity (not raw count) for the base clustering, quarantine wide/low-traffic components onto their own card instead of trusting affinity blindly, and spend the replication budget on the components whose braid collapses the most order-weight from a two-card cover down to one card
